subject: [PATCH 13/24] nfsd: add notification handlers for dir events

reviews.author: Chuck Lever
reviews.summary: Reviewer noted that the current implementation fails to send notifications for cross-directory renames due to incorrect flag handling, specifically failing to check FL_IGN_DIR_RENAME instead of FL_IGN_DIR_DELETE in should_notify_deleg().
reviews.raw_body:
For a cross-directory rename, vfs_rename calls try_break_deleg(old_dir,
LEASE_BREAK_DIR_DELETE, ...). A delegation with FL_IGN_DIR_DELETE
(subscribed to NOTIFY4_REMOVE_ENTRY) suppresses the lease break, which
is correct.

But fsnotify delivers FS_RENAME on old_dir, not FS_DELETE. In
should_notify_deleg(), the check (mask & FS_RENAME) &&
!(fl->c.flc_flags & FL_IGN_DIR_RENAME) fails, because the delegation
has FL_IGN_DIR_DELETE but not FL_IGN_DIR_RENAME. No notification is
sent.

IIUC, a client subscribed to NOTIFY4_REMOVE_ENTRY for old_dir sees
neither a lease break nor a CB_NOTIFY when a child is renamed out of
the directory. Is that behavior correct?

reviews.author: Jeff Layton (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledged that the patch needs to handle FS_RENAME_FROM and FS_RENAME_TO events in addition to FS_CREATE/DELETE, agreed to add a fix for this issue.
sentiment_signals: acknowledged a need for a fix
reviews.raw_body:
Actually, it delivers FS_RENAME_FROM to the old directory and
FS_RENAME_TO to the new one in this case, AFAICT. Basically, we need to
watch for those in addition to FS_CREATE/DELETE and treat them the same
way. I'm looking at a fix now. I'll also see about adding a pynfs test
to do a cross directory rename with notifications. I don't think I have
that now.

Thanks!

2026-04-07.patch_summary: This patch adds support for directory change events in the NFS server (nfsd). It introduces a new fsnotify group for directories and modifies existing code to use this new group when handling file marks. The patch also includes changes to handle notifications for delegation callbacks and to allocate nfsd_notify_event objects. If any errors occur during these operations, affected delegations are recalled. This patch is part of a larger series of 24 patches.
